definitions.definition: The central part of an atom that contains protons and neutrons. It is positively charged due to the presence of protons.
definitions.example: The nucleus of an atom is surrounded by electrons.

definitions.definition: A positively charged subatomic particle present in the nucleus of an atom. The number of protons in an atom's nucleus determines the atomic number and the identity of the element.
definitions.example: A proton has a positive charge equal to the charge of an electron but with a mass approximately 1836 times that of an electron.

definitions.definition: A subatomic particle present in the nucleus of an atom with no electric charge. It has a mass nearly equal to that of a proton.
definitions.example: Neutrons play a crucial role in the stability of an atomic nucleus.

definitions.definition: The total number of protons and neutrons present in the nucleus of an atom, also known as the atomic mass or nucleon number.
definitions.example: The mass number of an atom is denoted by the symbol A.

definitions.definition: The number of protons present in the nucleus of an atom, which determines the identity of an element.
definitions.example: The atomic number of an atom is denoted by the symbol Z.

definitions.definition: The process by which unstable atomic nuclei lose energy by emitting radiation in the form of alpha, beta, or gamma rays.
definitions.example: Radioactivity is a spontaneous process that occurs in certain isotopes.

definitions.definition: A type of radioactive decay where an alpha particle (two protons and two neutrons) is emitted from the nucleus of an atom.
definitions.example: Alpha decay is a common mode of radioactive decay for heavy elements.

definitions.definition: A type of radioactive decay where a beta particle (either a positron or an electron) is emitted from the nucleus of an atom.
definitions.example: Beta decay is a common mode of radioactive decay for certain isotopes.

definitions.definition: A type of radioactive decay where a gamma photon is emitted from the nucleus of an atom, often following alpha or beta decay.
definitions.example: Gamma decay is a common mode of radioactive decay for certain isotopes.

definitions.definition: Atoms of the same element with the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons, resulting in different mass numbers.
definitions.example: Isotopes of an element have the same chemical properties but different physical properties.

definitions.definition: Atoms of different elements with the same mass number but different numbers of protons, resulting in different atomic numbers.
definitions.example: Isobars have the same mass number but different chemical properties.

definitions.definition: An atom or molecule that has gained or lost electrons, resulting in a net positive or negative charge.
definitions.example: Ions are formed when atoms or molecules interact with other atoms or molecules.

definitions.definition: The energy required to disassemble an atomic nucleus into its constituent protons and neutrons.
definitions.example: Nuclear binding energy is a measure of the stability of an atomic nucleus.

definitions.definition: A process in which an atomic nucleus splits into two or more smaller nuclei, often accompanied by the release of energy.
definitions.example: Nuclear fission is a process used in nuclear reactors to generate electricity.

definitions.definition: A process in which two or more atomic nuclei combine to form a single, heavier nucleus, often accompanied by the release of energy.
definitions.example: Nuclear fusion is the process that powers the sun.

definitions.definition: A model of the atom proposed by Ernest Rutherford, in which the atom consists of a small, dense nucleus surrounded by electrons.
definitions.example: Rutherford's model of the atom was developed based on his gold foil experiment.

definitions.definition: A model of the atom proposed by Niels Bohr, in which the atom consists of a small, dense nucleus surrounded by electrons in specific energy levels.
definitions.example: Bohr's model of the atom was developed based on his work on atomic spectra.
